of England]] (1566–1625) ******''From whom succeeding English, Scottish and British monarchs are descended.'' ***[[:en:Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross|Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross]] (1514–1515) ***[[:en:Margaret Douglas|Margaret Douglas, Countess of Lennox]] (1515–1578) **[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(2)''' [[:en:Henry VIII of England|Henry VIII of England]] (1491–1547) ***[[:en:Henry, Duke of Cornwall|Henry, Duke of Cornwall]] (1511–1511) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(5)''' [[:en:Mary I of England|Mary I of England]] (1516–1558) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(6)''' [[:en:Elizabeth I of England|Elizabeth I of England]] (1533–1603) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(3)''' [[:en:Edward VI of England|Edward VI of England]] **[[Elizabeth Tudor (daughter of Henry VII)|Elizabeth Tudor]] (1492–1495) **[[Mary Tudor (queen consort of France)|Mary, Queen of France]] (1496–1533) ***Henry Brandon (1516–1522) ***[[:en:Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk|Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk]] (1517–1547) ****[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(4)''' [[:en:Lady Jane Grey|Lady Jane Grey]] (1536–1553) ****[[:en:Lady Catherine Grey|Catherine Seymour, Countess of Hertford]] (1540–1568) ****[[:en:Lady Mary Grey|Lady Mary Keyes]] (1545–1578) ****Elizabeth Stokes (1555–1556) ***[[:en:Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland|Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland]] (1519–1547) ****''From whom are descended the [[:en:Earl of Derby|Earls of Derby]]'' ***[[:en:Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln|Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln]] (1523–1534) **[[:en:Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset|Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset]] (1499–1500) **[[Katherine Tudor (1503)|Katherine Tudor]] (1503–1503) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ===An ancestry tree=== [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] style trees can be can be displayed using this template, but usually for fewer than six generations the customised {{tlx|ahnentafel}} templates are clearer (see [[:en:Template:Ahnentafel/doc|Template:Ahnentafel/doc]]). There is also an ahnentafel template {{Tlx|Ahnentafel-tree}}, that is based on this one, that makes construction of this tree simpler. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Ancestry tree of Prince William, Duke of Cambridge|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *1. '''[[William, Prince of Wales]]''' (born 21 June 1982) **2. [[:en:Charles III|Charles III of the United Kingdom]] (born 14 November 1948) ***4. [[:en:Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|Prince Philip of Greece and Denmark]] (10 June 1921 – 9 April 2021) ****8. [[:en:Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark|Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark]] (20 January 1882 – 3 December 1944) ****9. [[:en:Princess Alice of Battenberg|Princess Alice of Battenberg]] (25 February 1885 – 5 December 1969) ***5. [[:en:Elizabeth II|Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom]] (21 April 1926 – 8 September 2022) ****10. [[:en:George VI|George VI of the United Kingdom]] (14 December 1895 – 6 February 1952) ****11. [[Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother|Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon]] (4 August 1900 – 30 March 2002) **3. [[:en:Diana, Princess of Wales|Lady Diana Spencer]] (1 July 1961 – 31 August 1997) ***6. [[:en:John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer|John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer]] (21 April 1926 – 29 March 1992) ****12. [[:en:Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer|Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer]] (23 May 1892 – 9 June 1975) ****13. [[:en:Cynthia Spencer, Countess Spencer|Lady Cynthia Hamilton]] (16 August 1897 – 4 December 1972) ***7. [[:en:Frances Shand Kydd|The Honourable Frances Roche]] (20 January 1936 – 3 June 2004) ****14. [[:en:Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy|Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy]] (15 May 1885 – 8 July 1955) ****15. [[Ruth Roche, Baroness Fermoy|Ruth Gill]] (2 October 1908 – 6 July 1993) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Ancestry tree of Prince William, Duke of Cambridge|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *1. '''[[William, Prince of Wales]]''' (born 21 June 1982) **2. [[:en:Charles III|Charles III of the United Kingdom]] (born 14 November 1948) ***4. [[:en:Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|Prince Philip of Greece and Denmark]] (10 June 1921 – 9 April 2021) ****8. [[:en:Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark|Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark]] (20 January 1882 – 3 December 1944) ****9. [[:en:Princess Alice of Battenberg|Princess Alice of Battenberg]] (25 February 1885 – 5 December 1969) ***5. [[:en:Elizabeth II|Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom]] (21 April 1926 – 8 September 2022) ****10. [[:en:George VI|George VI of the United Kingdom]] (14 December 1895 – 6 February 1952) ****11. [[Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother|Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon]] (4 August 1900 – 30 March 2002) **3. [[:en:Diana, Princess of Wales|Lady Diana Spencer]] (1 July 1961 – 31 August 1997) ***6. [[:en:John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer|John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer]] (21 April 1926 – 29 March 1992) ****12. [[:en:Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer|Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer]] (23 May 1892 – 9 June 1975) ****13. [[:en:Cynthia Spencer, Countess Spencer|Lady Cynthia Hamilton]] (16 August 1897 – 4 December 1972) ***7. [[:en:Frances Shand Kydd|The Honourable Frances Roche]] (20 January 1936 – 3 June 2004) ****14. [[:en:Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy|Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy]] (15 May 1885 – 8 July 1955) ****15. [[Ruth Roche, Baroness Fermoy|Ruth Gill]] (2 October 1908 – 6 July 1993) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} === A cladogram === {{tlx|Clade}} should be preferred, {{tlx|cladogram}} provides boxed cladograms, and {{tlx|cladex}} has extended cladogram parameters. Ask [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests|WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests]] for assistance. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Family trees]] * {{Tlx|Chart}}—This template produces one row in a "family tree"-like chart consisting of boxes and connecting lines * {{Tlx|Ahnentafel}}—This template takes genealogical data in the form of an [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] and presents it as a graphical ancestry tree * {{Tlx|ahnentafel-tree}} uses {{tlx|Tree list}} and will handle up to an ahnentafel of 511 places (9 generations), but will also display as little as one generation. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Genealogy formatting templates]] }}

Ask [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests|WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests]] for assistance. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Family trees]] * {{Tlx|Chart}}—This template produces one row in a "family tree"-like chart consisting of boxes and connecting lines * {{Tlx|Ahnentafel}}—This template takes genealogical data in the form of an [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] and presents it as a graphical ancestry tree * {{Tlx|ahnentafel-tree}} uses {{tlx|Tree list}} and will handle up to an ahnentafel of 511 places (9 generations), but will also display as little as one generation. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Genealogy formatting templates]] }}

revision.text: {{doc subpage}} {{High-use}} {{When on basepage|{{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tree list/styles.css}}}} {{redirect-distinguish|Template:Tree diagram|Template:Tree chart}} This family of templates can be used to create a properly structured and accessible tree. This can be used to display family trees, succession to thrones, etc. They comprise: {{Tree list}} * {{tl|Tree list}}/{{'''Tree list/start'''}} ** {{tl|Tree list/end}} ** {{tl|Tree list/branching}} {{Tree list/end}} == Syntax == === Start and finish of the tree === The tree must include a start and finish, using the templates {{tl|Tree list}} and {{tl|Tree list/end}} respectively. The tree is essentially a series of nested lists built with the asterisk *. {{Tree list}} * Here goes the content of the tree using asterisks. {{Tree list/end}} === The branches of the tree === Each branch of the tree is built using nested lists: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} === Unlabelled branches === An unlabelled branch may be formed by using the {{tl|Tree list/branching}} template. {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces the following: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} ==Collapse== To collapse a tree place {{tlx|Hidden begin}} before {{tlx|Tree list}} and enclose {{tlx|Tree list/end}} with {{tlx|Hidden end}} (see [[:en:MOS:DONTHIDE|MOS:DONTHIDE]] before hiding content in articles). {{tlx|Hidden begin}} defaults to just a show link on the right, but it takes a number of parameters, see the template's documentation. In this case: |border=1px #aaa solid—a solid border around the tree |title=Demonstration tree—a suitable name for the tree |ta1=center – center the title= name {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Demonstration tree|ta1=center}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|ta1=center||title=Demonstration tree}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} If the parameter |expanded=on is added to {{tlx|hidden begin}} then the box will be drawn expanded rather than the default, which is to collapse the tree. == Examples == ===A family tree === {{see also|Wikipedia:Family trees}} '''Code:''' {{hidden '''[[:en:William, Prince of Wales|William, Prince of Wales]]''' (born 21 June 1982) **2. [[:en:Charles III|Charles III of the United Kingdom]] (born 14 November 1948) ***4. [[:en:Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|Prince Philip of Greece and Denmark]] (10 June 1921 – 9 April 2021) ****8. [[:en:Ruth Roche, Baroness Fermoy|Ruth Gill]] (2 October 1908 – 6 July 1993) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} === A cladogram === {{tlx|Clade}} should be preferred, {{tlx|cladogram}} provides boxed cladograms, and {{tlx|cladex}} has extended cladogram parameters. Ask [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests|WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests]] for assistance. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Family trees]] * {{Tlx|Chart}}—This template produces one row in a "family tree"-like chart consisting of boxes and connecting lines * {{Tlx|Ahnentafel}}—This template takes genealogical data in the form of an [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] and presents it as a graphical ancestry tree * {{Tlx|ahnentafel-tree}} uses {{tlx|Tree list}} and will handle up to an ahnentafel of 511 places (9 generations), but will also display as little as one generation. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Genealogy formatting templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{doc subpage}} {{High-use}} {{When on basepage|{{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tree list/styles.css}}}} {{redirect-distinguish|Template:Tree diagram|Template:Tree chart}} This family of templates can be used to create a properly structured and accessible tree. This can be used to display family trees, succession to thrones, etc. They comprise: {{Tree list}} * {{tl|Tree list}}/{{'''Tree list/start'''}} ** {{tl|Tree list/end}} ** {{tl|Tree list/branching}} {{Tree list/end}} == Syntax == === Start and finish of the tree === The tree must include a start and finish, using the templates {{tl|Tree list}} and {{tl|Tree list/end}} respectively. The tree is essentially a series of nested lists built with the asterisk *. {{Tree list}} * Here goes the content of the tree using asterisks. {{Tree list/end}} === The branches of the tree === Each branch of the tree is built using nested lists: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} === Unlabelled branches === An unlabelled branch may be formed by using the {{tl|Tree list/branching}} template. {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces the following: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} ==Collapse== To collapse a tree place {{tlx|Hidden begin}} before {{tlx|Tree list}} and enclose {{tlx|Tree list/end}} with {{tlx|Hidden end}} (see [[:en:MOS:DONTHIDE|MOS:DONTHIDE]] before hiding content in articles). {{tlx|Hidden begin}} defaults to just a show link on the right, but it takes a number of parameters, see the template's documentation. In this case: |border=1px #aaa solid—a solid border around the tree |title=Demonstration tree—a suitable name for the tree |ta1=center – center the title= name {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Demonstration tree|ta1=center}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|ta1=center||title=Demonstration tree}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} If the parameter |expanded=on is added to {{tlx|hidden begin}} then the box will be drawn expanded rather than the default, which is to collapse the tree. == Examples == ===A family tree === {{see also|Wikipedia:Family trees}} '''Code:''' {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A family tree (descendants of Henry VII of England)|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(1)''' [[:en:Henry VII of England|Henry VII]] (1457–1509) **[[:en:Arthur, Prince of Wales|Arthur, Prince of Wales]] (1486–1502) **[[:en:Margaret Tudor|Margaret, Queen of Scots]] (1489–1541) ***[[:en:James, Duke of Rothesay|James, Duke of Rothesay]] (1507–1508) ***[[:en:Arthur, Duke of Rothesay|Arthur, Duke of Rothesay]] (1509–1510) ***[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:James V of Scotland|James V of Scotland]] (1512–1542) ****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:Mary, Queen of Scots|Mary, Queen of Scots]] (1542–1587) *****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[Image:Simple gold ***[[:en:Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross|Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross]] (1514–1515) ***[[:en:Margaret Douglas|Margaret Douglas, Countess of Lennox]] (1515–1578) **[[Image:Simple gold revision.text: {{doc subpage}} {{High-use}} {{When on basepage|{{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tree list/styles.css}}}} {{redirect-distinguish|Template:Tree diagram|Template:Tree chart}} This family of templates can be used to create a properly structured and accessible tree. This can be used to display family trees, succession to thrones, etc. They comprise: {{Tree list}} * {{tl|Tree list}}/{{'''Tree list/start'''}} ** {{tl|Tree list/end}} ** {{tl|Tree list/branching}} {{Tree list/end}} == Syntax == === Start and finish of the tree === The tree must include a start and finish, using the templates {{tl|Tree list}} and {{tl|Tree list/end}} respectively. The tree is essentially a series of nested lists built with the asterisk *. {{Tree list}} * Here goes the content of the tree using asterisks. {{Tree list/end}} === The branches of the tree === Each branch of the tree is built using nested lists: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} === Unlabelled branches === An unlabelled branch may be formed by using the {{tl|Tree list/branching}} template. {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces the following: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} ==Collapse== To collapse a tree place {{tlx|Hidden begin}} before {{tlx|Tree list}} and enclose {{tlx|Tree list/end}} with {{tlx|Hidden end}} (see [[:en:MOS:DONTHIDE|MOS:DONTHIDE]] before hiding content in articles). {{tlx|Hidden begin}} defaults to just a show link on the right, but it takes a number of parameters, see the template's documentation. In this case: |border=1px #aaa solid—a solid border around the tree |title=Demonstration tree—a suitable name for the tree |ta1=center – center the title= name {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Demonstration tree|ta1=center}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|ta1=center||title=Demonstration tree}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} If the parameter |expanded=on is added to {{tlx|hidden begin}} then the box will be drawn expanded rather than the default, which is to collapse the tree. == Examples == ===A family tree === {{see also|Wikipedia:Family trees}} '''Code:''' {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A family tree (descendants of Henry VII of England)|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(1)''' [[:en:Henry VII of England|Henry VII]] (1457–1509) **[[:en:Arthur, Prince of Wales|Arthur, Prince of Wales]] (1486–1502) **[[:en:Margaret Tudor|Margaret, Queen of Scots]] (1489–1541) ***[[:en:James, Duke of Rothesay|James, Duke of Rothesay]] (1507–1508) ***[[:en:Arthur, Duke of Rothesay|Arthur, Duke of Rothesay]] (1509–1510) ***[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:James V of Scotland|James V of Scotland]] (1512–1542) ****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:Mary, Queen of Scots|Mary, Queen of Scots]] (1542–1587) *****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(7)''' [[:en:James VI and I|James VI of Scotland and I of England]] (1566–1625) ******''From whom succeeding English, Scottish and British monarchs are descended.'' ***[[:en:Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross|Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross]] (1514–1515) ***[[:en:Margaret Douglas|Margaret Douglas, Countess of Lennox]] (1515–1578) **[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(2)''' [[:en:Henry VIII of England|Henry VIII of England]] (1491–1547) ***[[:en:Henry, Duke of Cornwall|Henry, Duke of Cornwall]] (1511–1511) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(5)''' [[:en:Mary I of England|Mary I of England]] (1516–1558) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(6)''' [[:en:Elizabeth I of England|Elizabeth I of England]] (1533–1603) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(3)''' [[:en:Edward VI of England|Edward VI of England]] **[[Elizabeth Tudor (daughter of Henry VII)|Elizabeth Tudor]] (1492–1495) **[[Mary Tudor (queen consort of France)|Mary, Queen of France]] (1496–1533) ***Henry Brandon (1516–1522) ***[[:en:Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk|Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk]] (1517–1547) ****[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(4)''' [[:en:Lady Jane Grey|Lady Jane Grey]] (1536–1553) ****[[:en:Lady Catherine Grey|Catherine Seymour, Countess of Hertford]] (1540–1568) ****[[:en:Lady Mary Grey|Lady Mary Keyes]] (1545–1578) ****Elizabeth Stokes (1555–1556) ***[[:en:Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland|Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland]] (1519–1547) ****''From whom are descended the [[:en:Earl of Derby|Earls of Derby]]'' ***[[:en:Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln|Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln]] (1523–1534) **[[:en:Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset|Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset]] (1499–1500) **[[Katherine Tudor (1503)|Katherine Tudor]] (1503–1503) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} which gives: {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A family tree (descendants of Henry VII of England) |ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(1)''' [[:en:Henry VII of England|Henry VII]] (1457–1509) **[[:en:Arthur, Prince of Wales|Arthur, Prince of Wales]] (1486–1502) **[[:en:Margaret Tudor|Margaret, Queen of Scots]] (1489–1541) ***[[:en:James, Duke of Rothesay|James, Duke of Rothesay]] (1507–1508) ***[[:en:Arthur, Duke of Rothesay|Arthur, Duke of Rothesay]] (1509–1510) ***[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:James V of Scotland|James V of Scotland]] (1512–1542) ****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:Mary, Queen of Scots|Mary, Queen of Scots]] (1542–1587) *****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(7)''' [[:en:James VI and I|James VI of Scotland and I of England]] (1566–1625) ******''From whom succeeding English, Scottish and British monarchs are descended.'' ***[[:en:Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross|Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross]] (1514–1515) ***[[:en:Margaret Douglas|Margaret Douglas, Countess of Lennox]] (1515–1578) **[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(2)''' [[:en:Henry VIII of England|Henry VIII of England]] (1491–1547) ***[[:en:Henry, Duke of Cornwall|Henry, Duke of Cornwall]] (1511–1511) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(5)''' [[:en:Mary I of England|Mary I of England]] (1516–1558) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(6)''' [[:en:Elizabeth I of England|Elizabeth I of England]] (1533–1603) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(3)''' [[:en:Edward VI of England|Edward VI of England]] **[[Elizabeth Tudor (daughter of Henry VII)|Elizabeth Tudor]] (1492–1495) **[[Mary Tudor (queen consort of France)|Mary, Queen of France]] (1496–1533) ***Henry Brandon (1516–1522) ***[[:en:Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk|Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk]] (1517–1547) ****[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(4)''' [[:en:Lady Jane Grey|Lady Jane Grey]] (1536–1553) ****[[:en:Lady Catherine Grey|Catherine Seymour, Countess of Hertford]] (1540–1568) ****[[:en:Lady Mary Grey|Lady Mary Keyes]] (1545–1578) ****Elizabeth Stokes (1555–1556) ***[[:en:Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland|Eleanor Clifford, Countess of Cumberland]] (1519–1547) ****''From whom are descended the [[:en:Earl of Derby|Earls of Derby]]'' ***[[:en:Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln|Henry Brandon, 1st Earl of Lincoln]] (1523–1534) **[[:en:Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset|Edmund Tudor, Duke of Somerset]] (1499–1500) **[[Katherine Tudor (1503)|Katherine Tudor]] (1503–1503) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ===An ancestry tree=== [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] style trees can be can be displayed using this template, but usually for fewer than six generations the customised {{tlx|ahnentafel}} templates are clearer (see [[:en:Template:Ahnentafel/doc|Template:Ahnentafel/doc]]). There is also an ahnentafel template {{Tlx|Ahnentafel-tree}}, that is based on this one, that makes construction of this tree simpler. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Ancestry tree of Prince William, Duke of Cambridge|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *1. '''[[:en:William, Prince of Wales|William, Prince of Wales]]''' (born 21 June 1982) **2. [[:en:Charles III|Charles III of the United Kingdom]] (born 14 November 1948) ***4. [[:en:Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|Prince Philip of Greece and Denmark]] (10 June 1921 – 9 April 2021) ****8. [[:en:Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark|Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark]] (20 January 1882 – 3 December 1944) ****9. [[:en:Princess Alice of Battenberg|Princess Alice of Battenberg]] (25 February 1885 – 5 December 1969) ***5. [[:en:Elizabeth II|Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom]] (21 April 1926 – 8 September 2022) ****10. [[:en:George VI|George VI of the United Kingdom]] (14 December 1895 – 6 February 1952) ****11. [[:en:Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother|Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon]] (4 August 1900 – 30 March 2002) **3. [[:en:Diana, Princess of Wales|Lady Diana Spencer]] (1 July 1961 – 31 August 1997) ***6. [[:en:John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer|John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer]] (21 April 1926 – 29 March 1992) ****12. [[:en:Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer|Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer]] (23 May 1892 – 9 June 1975) ****13. [[:en:Cynthia Spencer, Countess Spencer|Lady Cynthia Hamilton]] (16 August 1897 – 4 December 1972) ***7. [[:en:Frances Shand Kydd|The Honourable Frances Roche]] (20 January 1936 – 3 June 2004) ****14. [[:en:Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy|Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy]] (15 May 1885 – 8 July 1955) ****15. [[:en:Ruth Roche, Baroness Fermoy|Ruth Gill]] (2 October 1908 – 6 July 1993) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Ancestry tree of Prince William, Duke of Cambridge|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *1. '''[[:en:William, Prince of Wales|William, Prince of Wales]]''' (born 21 June 1982) **2. [[:en:Charles III|Charles III of the United Kingdom]] (born 14 November 1948) ***4. [[:en:Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh|Prince Philip of Greece and Denmark]] (10 June 1921 – 9 April 2021) ****8. [[:en:Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark|Prince Andrew of Greece and Denmark]] (20 January 1882 – 3 December 1944) ****9. [[:en:Princess Alice of Battenberg|Princess Alice of Battenberg]] (25 February 1885 – 5 December 1969) ***5. [[:en:Elizabeth II|Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom]] (21 April 1926 – 8 September 2022) ****10. [[:en:George VI|George VI of the United Kingdom]] (14 December 1895 – 6 February 1952) ****11. [[:en:Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother|Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon]] (4 August 1900 – 30 March 2002) **3. [[:en:Diana, Princess of Wales|Lady Diana Spencer]] (1 July 1961 – 31 August 1997) ***6. [[:en:John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer|John Spencer, 8th Earl Spencer]] (21 April 1926 – 29 March 1992) ****12. [[:en:Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer|Albert Spencer, 7th Earl Spencer]] (23 May 1892 – 9 June 1975) ****13. [[:en:Cynthia Spencer, Countess Spencer|Lady Cynthia Hamilton]] (16 August 1897 – 4 December 1972) ***7. [[:en:Frances Shand Kydd|The Honourable Frances Roche]] (20 January 1936 – 3 June 2004) ****14. [[:en:Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy|Maurice Roche, 4th Baron Fermoy]] (15 May 1885 – 8 July 1955) ****15. [[:en:Ruth Roche, Baroness Fermoy|Ruth Gill]] (2 October 1908 – 6 July 1993) {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} === A cladogram === {{tlx|Clade}} should be preferred, {{tlx|cladogram}} provides boxed cladograms, and {{tlx|cladex}} has extended cladogram parameters. Ask [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests|WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests]] for assistance. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Family trees]] * {{Tlx|Chart}}—This template produces one row in a "family tree"-like chart consisting of boxes and connecting lines * {{Tlx|Ahnentafel}}—This template takes genealogical data in the form of an [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] and presents it as a graphical ancestry tree * {{Tlx|ahnentafel-tree}} uses {{tlx|Tree list}} and will handle up to an ahnentafel of 511 places (9 generations), but will also display as little as one generation. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Genealogy formatting templates]] }}

Ask [[Wikipedia:WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests|WikiProject Tree of Life/Cladogram requests]] for assistance. {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A cladogram|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[:en:Tetrapoda|Tetrapoda]] ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups such as the ''[[:en:Eucritta|Eucritta]]'' *** '''[[:en:Lissamphibia|Lissamphibia]]''' Whose ancestors of the current amphibians ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** Many extinct groups reminiscent of amphibians with more or less pronounced reptilian characteristics such as [[:en:Eoherpetontidae|Eoherpetontidae]], the [[:en:Embolomeri|Embolomeri]], the [[Tokosauridae]], [[:en:Chroniosuchidae|Chroniosuchidae]], [[:en:Gephyrostegidae|Gephyrostegidae]], [[Solenodonsauridae]], [[:en:Seymouriamorpha|Seymouriamorpha]], [[:en:Westlothiana|Westlothiana]], [[:en:Lepospondyli|Lepospondyli]] *** [[:en:Cotylosauria|Cotylosauria]] **** [[:en:Amniota|Amniota]], Originally all "Reptilian" **** Other extinct sibling taxa like [[:en:Diadectomorpha|Diadectomorpha]] or [[:en:Tseajaiidae|Tseajaiidae]] are close enough to reptiles {{Tree list/end}} {{hidden end}} ==See also== * [[Wikipedia:Family trees]] * {{Tlx|Chart}}—This template produces one row in a "family tree"-like chart consisting of boxes and connecting lines * {{Tlx|Ahnentafel}}—This template takes genealogical data in the form of an [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] and presents it as a graphical ancestry tree * {{Tlx|ahnentafel-tree}} uses {{tlx|Tree list}} and will handle up to an ahnentafel of 511 places (9 generations), but will also display as little as one generation. {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:List formatting and function templates]] [[Category:Genealogy formatting templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.text: {{doc subpage}} {{High-use}} {{When on basepage|{{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Tree list/styles.css}}}} {{redirect-distinguish|Template:Tree diagram|Template:Tree chart}} This family of templates can be used to create a properly structured and accessible tree. This can be used to display family trees, succession to thrones, etc. They comprise: {{Tree list}} * {{tl|Tree list}}/{{'''Tree list/start'''}} ** {{tl|Tree list/end}} ** {{tl|Tree list/branching}} {{Tree list/end}} == Syntax == === Start and finish of the tree === The tree must include a start and finish, using the templates {{tl|Tree list}} and {{tl|Tree list/end}} respectively. The tree is essentially a series of nested lists built with the asterisk *. {{Tree list}} * Here goes the content of the tree using asterisks. {{Tree list/end}} === The branches of the tree === Each branch of the tree is built using nested lists: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** A second level branch *** A third level branch {{Tree list/end}} === Unlabelled branches === An unlabelled branch may be formed by using the {{tl|Tree list/branching}} template. {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} produces the following: {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} ==Collapse== To collapse a tree place {{tlx|Hidden begin}} before {{tlx|Tree list}} and enclose {{tlx|Tree list/end}} with {{tlx|Hidden end}} (see [[:en:MOS:DONTHIDE|MOS:DONTHIDE]] before hiding content in articles). {{tlx|Hidden begin}} defaults to just a show link on the right, but it takes a number of parameters, see the template's documentation. In this case: |border=1px #aaa solid—a solid border around the tree |title=Demonstration tree—a suitable name for the tree |ta1=center – center the title= name {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=Demonstration tree|ta1=center}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|ta1=center||title=Demonstration tree}} {{Tree list}} * A first level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** Another third level branch *** The final third level branch ** A second level branch ** {{Tree list/branching}} *** A third level branch *** The final third level branch {{Tree list/end}} {{Hidden end}} If the parameter |expanded=on is added to {{tlx|hidden begin}} then the box will be drawn expanded rather than the default, which is to collapse the tree. == Examples == ===A family tree === {{see also|Wikipedia:Family trees}} '''Code:''' {{hidden begin|border=1px #aaa solid|title=A family tree (descendants of Henry VII of England)|ta1=center|expanded=on}} {{Tree list}} *[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(1)''' [[:en:Henry VII of England|Henry VII]] (1457–1509) **[[:en:Arthur, Prince of Wales|Arthur, Prince of Wales]] (1486–1502) **[[:en:Margaret Tudor|Margaret, Queen of Scots]] (1489–1541) ***[[:en:James, Duke of Rothesay|James, Duke of Rothesay]] (1507–1508) ***[[:en:Arthur, Duke of Rothesay|Arthur, Duke of Rothesay]] (1509–1510) ***[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:James V of Scotland|James V of Scotland]] (1512–1542) ****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[:en:Mary, Queen of Scots|Mary, Queen of Scots]] (1542–1587) *****[[Image:Simple silver crown.svg|15px]] [[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(7)''' [[:en:James VI and I|James VI of Scotland and I of England]] (1566–1625) ******''From whom succeeding English, Scottish and British monarchs are descended.'' ***[[:en:Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross|Alexander Stewart, Duke of Ross]] (1514–1515) ***[[:en:Margaret Douglas|Margaret Douglas, Countess of Lennox]] (1515–1578) **[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(2)''' [[:en:Henry VIII of England|Henry VIII of England]] (1491–1547) ***[[:en:Henry, Duke of Cornwall|Henry, Duke of Cornwall]] (1511–1511) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(5)''' [[:en:Mary I of England|Mary I of England]] (1516–1558) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(6)''' [[:en:Elizabeth I of England|Elizabeth I of England]] (1533–1603) ***[[Image:Simple gold crown.svg|15px]] '''(3)''' [[:en:Edward VI of England|Edward VI of England]] **[[Elizabeth Tudor (daughter of Henry VII)|Elizabeth Tudor]] (1492–1495) **[[Mary Tudor (queen consort of France)|Mary, Queen of France]] (1496–1533) ***Henry Brandon (1516–1522) ***[[:en:Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk|Frances Grey, Duchess of Suffolk]] (1517–1547) ****[[Image:Simple gold {{hidden end}} ===An ancestry tree=== [[:en:Ahnentafel|Ahnentafel]] style trees can be can be displayed using this template, but usually for fewer than six generations the customised {{tlx|ahnentafel}} templates are clearer (see [[:en:Template:Ahnentafel/doc|Template:Ahnentafel/doc]]).
